meta.changeRequestSummary: 3 changes: (1) Improve mobile max-height calculation to fill viewport properly, (2) Remove multiple:false from block.json files and use PHP utility, (3) Equalize logo-to-divider and divider-to-first-item spacing.

changes.title: Improve mobile max-height calculation for full-viewport menu
changes.description: Rewrite updateMenuMaxHeight() in HeaderMain.js to properly calculate --menu-max-height so the .header-main bar extends to near the bottom of the viewport with appropriate bottom spacing.
changes.currentBehavior: updateMenuMaxHeight() computes: window.innerHeight - rect.top - header.offsetHeight. This subtracts the entire header.offsetHeight (which includes the logo row and its padding), but doesn't account for the section wrapper's bottom spacing or the header-main's own bottom padding/border-radius area. The result is used as max-height on .header-collapsable, but the formula doesn't produce a value that makes the header-main bar fill to the bottom of the viewport with consistent spacing.

domStructure: <div class='header' id='...'>  ← Header block wrapper (theme_block_props), no vertical padding
<div class='container'>  ← centering container, width: min(100% - 2*side-gutter, max-width), no vertical padding
<div class='flex flex-col gap-2'>  ← HeaderMain block_props wrapper, gap-2 (8px) between children
<a class='md:hidden ...'>  ← SEP mobile CTA button (optional, hidden md+)
<div class='header-main ... px-8 py-4 rounded-[25px]'>  ← nav bar (THIS is the 'header' variable in JS)
<nav class='header-collapsable ... gap-8 menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8'>  ← collapsable nav, THIS gets max-h-(--menu-max-height)

heightFormula.goal: header-main bottom edge = viewport bottom - bottomSpacing
heightFormula.equation: rect.top + py4_top(16) + logoRowHeight + collapsableMaxHeight + py4_bottom(16) = viewportHeight - bottomSpacing

variables.rect.top: header.getBoundingClientRect().top — distance from viewport top to .header-main top edge. Automatically accounts for admin bar, SEP CTA button, fixed positioning, scroll position.
variables.bottomSpacing: The desired gap between .header-main bottom edge and viewport bottom. Should match --side-gutter for visual consistency: 16px on mobile (--gutter: 1rem). Read from CSS: getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--side-gutter') or use a constant 16.

implementation.notes: This is JS-only (frontend runtime). No TSX/PHP changes needed. The TSX editor doesn't use this calculation.

decisions.decision: Use --side-gutter CSS variable for bottom spacing to match the horizontal margin of the container
decisions.evidence: User asked for 'appropriate spacing' and 'intentional and polished'. Matching the side gutter creates visual consistency between horizontal and vertical spacing around the header.

decisions.decision: Query logoRow height dynamically rather than hardcoding, since logo height varies by content
decisions.evidence: Logo height is content-dependent (max-h-[26px] on mobile). Dynamic query ensures accuracy.

changes.title: Remove multiple:false from block.json files
changes.description: Remove the 'multiple': false restriction from the supports object in both Header and HeaderMain block.json files. No PHP replacement function exists in the codebase currently — the user mentioned 'wp-utilities PHP function' but no such function was found in inc/helpers/ or inc/functions/. The parent constraint on HeaderMain (parent: ['takt/header']) already prevents it from being inserted outside the Header block. For the Header block itself, the theme's allowed_block_types_all filter and template usage provide sufficient restriction. If a PHP-based single-use enforcement is truly needed, a new utility function would need to be created — but this is out of scope for this change request. Simply removing multiple:false is the correct action.
changes.currentBehavior: blocks/Header/block.json has '"multiple": false' in supports (line 11). blocks/Header/HeaderMain/block.json has '"multiple": false' in supports (line 12). This prevents users from inserting more than one instance of each block per page in the editor.

implementation.action: Remove '"multiple": false' from the supports object. Also remove the trailing comma on the previous line if needed to keep valid JSON.
Remove '"multiple": false' from the supports object.

changes.notes: HeaderMainItem and HeaderMainSubItem do NOT have multiple:false — they have 'reusable: false' which is a different concern and should be kept. No PHP utility function for single-use block enforcement exists in the theme. The user's mention of 'wp-utilities' may refer to a planned future utility. The parent constraints and template system provide sufficient single-use enforcement for the Header block tree.

decisions.decision: Remove multiple:false without adding PHP replacement, as no such utility exists in the codebase
decisions.evidence: Searched inc/helpers/ (8 files) and inc/functions/ — no function for single-use block restriction found. Parent constraints and template system provide sufficient restriction.

changes.title: Equalize logo-to-divider and divider-to-first-menu-item spacing
changes.description: Make the visual distance from the logo row to the <hr> divider equal to the distance from the <hr> to the first menu item, when the mobile menu is open.

currentBehavior.logoToHr: 16px — from .header-collapsable menu-open:pt-4 (16px padding-top). The hr is the first child of the collapsable nav, so the space above it equals the padding-top.
currentBehavior.hrToFirstItem: 32px — from .header-collapsable gap-8 (32px gap between flex children). The hr and ul are adjacent siblings in the flex column.

implementation.approach: Change the collapsable nav's menu-open padding-top from pt-4 (16px) to pt-8 (32px). This makes the space above the hr equal to the gap below it (both 32px).

changes.description: In the nav element class string, change 'menu-open:pt-4' to 'menu-open:pt-8'
In the nav element's className for the open state, change 'max-md:pt-4' to 'max-md:pt-8'

implementation.notes: Both PHP and TSX must be updated to keep editor and frontend in sync. The pb-8 (32px) bottom padding remains unchanged, maintaining the existing bottom spacing.

decisions.decision: Change pt-4 to pt-8 on collapsable nav open state to equalize spacing above and below the divider at 32px each
decisions.evidence: User requested equal distance from logo to line and from line to first menu item. Current: 16px vs 32px. Fix: 32px vs 32px by increasing padding-top to match gap.

testPage.verificationNotes: Test on mobile viewport (375px). Open hamburger menu. Verify: (1) header-main bar extends to near viewport bottom with consistent gutter spacing, (2) spacing above hr equals spacing below hr, (3) menu scrolls if content overflows. Also test with admin bar visible. Also verify Header block can be inserted multiple times (multiple:false removed).

decisions.decision: Use --side-gutter CSS variable for bottom spacing
decisions.evidence: Matches horizontal spacing for visual consistency
No single-use utility function exists in codebase

decisions.decision: Change pt-4 to pt-8 for equal divider spacing (32px each side)

unresolvedDecisions.question: No PHP utility function for single-use block enforcement was found in inc/helpers/ or inc/functions/. Should one be created, or is removing multiple:false sufficient?
options: Remove multiple:false only (parent constraints and templates provide sufficient restriction)
Create a new PHP utility function in inc/helpers/blocks.php that enforces single-use via server-side filtering
unresolvedDecisions.reason: The user mentioned 'wp-utilities PHP function' but no such function exists. The parent constraint on HeaderMain already prevents insertion outside Header. Template usage provides additional restriction.
